args.command: mkdir -p /workspace/research /workspace/decisions && echo "Workspace ready"

cat > /workspace/research/notes.md << 'NOTES'
# Research Notes — Password Manager Evaluation

## Context
- 50-person engineering team, Series-B SaaS company
- Current state: shadow IT (browser, sticky notes, Slack DMs)
- CTO mandate: fix within 90 days
- Need: team-wide standardization on a single password manager

## Candidate Products (to evaluate)
1. 1Password
2. Bitwarden
3. LastPass
4. Dashlane
5. Keeper Security

## Key Criteria (to be refined)
- SSO integration (Google Workspace / Okta)
- Admin console & audit logging (SOC 2 compliance)
- Pricing transparency & per-seat cost
- Mobile + desktop + browser coverage
- Developer tooling (CLI, API, CI/CD integration)
- Security model (zero-knowledge, encryption)
- Vendor lock-in / export capability
- Incident history

NOTES
echo "Notes initialized"

curl -sL "https://1password.com/pricing/" | head -500 > /tmp/1pw_pricing.html 2>&1
echo "1Password pricing page: $(wc -c < /tmp/1pw_pricing.html) bytes"

# Extract pricing info from 1Password page
grep -i -E 'price|per user|per seat|business|teams|enterprise|\$[0-9]' /tmp/1pw_pricing.html | head -40

# Try to get 1Password pricing from their business page
curl -sL "https://1password.com/business/" 2>/dev/null | grep -oP '\$[0-9]+(?:\.[0-9]+)?(?:/mo|/month|/user|/seat|/yr|/year)' | head -20
echo "---"
# Also try their teams pricing
curl -sL "https://1password.com/pricing/teams/" 2>/dev/null | grep -oP '\$[0-9]+(?:\.[0-9]+)?(?:/mo|/month|/user|/seat|/yr|/year)' | head -20

# Let me try a more targeted search for 1Password pricing
curl -sL "https://1password.com/pricing/" 2>/dev/null | grep -oP '[0-9]+(?:\.[0-9]+)?' | sort -u | head -30
echo "==="
# Try to find pricing in the JSON data
curl -sL "https://1password.com/pricing/" 2>/dev/null | grep -oP '"price"[^}]*' | head -20

# Get more context around the business/team pricing
curl -sL "https://1password.com/pricing/" 2>/dev/null | grep -oP '"price":"[^"]*"[^}]*' | head -30
echo "==="
# Look for business/enterprise tier names
curl -sL "https://1password.com/pricing/" 2>/dev/null | grep -oP '"name":"[^"]*"' | grep -i -E 'business|team|enterprise|family|individual' | head -20

# Get tier names and features
curl -sL "https://1password.com/pricing/" 2>/dev/null | grep -oP '"tierName":"[^"]*"' | head -20
echo "==="
curl -sL "https://1password.com/pricing/" 2>/dev/null | grep -oP '"title":"[^"]*"' | head -30

# Let me look at the 1Password business pricing more specifically
# The $9.99/user/month and $7.99/user/month (annual) look like business pricing
# Let me also check their business page
curl -sL "https://1password.com/business/" 2>/dev/null | grep -oP '"price":"[^"]*"[^}]*' | head -20
echo "==="
# Check for SSO, admin, audit mentions
curl -sL "https://1password.com/business/" 2>/dev/null | grep -oiP 'SSO|SAML|admin|audit|SOC 2|zero.knowledge' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -20

# Let me try a different approach - use a text-mode browser or look at cached versions
# First, let me check 1Password's trust/security page
curl -sL "https://1password.com/trust/" 2>/dev/null | grep -oiP 'SOC 2|FedRAMP|ISO|penetration test|audit' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -20
echo "=== 1Password security page ==="
curl -sL "https://1password.com/security/" 2>/dev/null | grep -oiP 'SOC 2|FedRAMP|ISO|penetration test|audit|zero.knowledge' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -20

# Let me use a Python script to extract structured pricing data from 1Password
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import urllib.request
import re
import json

url = "https://1password.com/pricing/"
req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36"})
html = urllib.request.urlopen(req).read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')

# Find all price blocks
prices = re.findall(r'"price":"([^"]*)"[^}]*"postPriceDetails":"([^"]*)"', html)
for p, d in prices:
    if 'user' in d.lower() or 'Per user' in d:
        print(f"  {p} — {d}")

print("\n--- All prices ---")
for p, d in prices:
    print(f"  {p} — {d}")
PYEOF

# Now let me get the tier names from 1Password
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import urllib.request, re

url = "https://1password.com/pricing/"
req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36"})
html = urllib.request.urlopen(req).read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')

# Look for plan/tier names near pricing
# Search for patterns like "Business", "Teams", "Enterprise" near price data
matches = re.findall(r'"(Business|Teams|Enterprise|Family|Individual|Personal)[^"]*"', html)
print("Tier-related strings:", set(matches))

# Look for plan names in a broader way
plan_names = re.findall(r'"(plan|tier|name|label)"\s*:\s*"([^"]*)"', html)
for k, v in plan_names:
    if any(x in v.lower() for x in ['business', 'team', 'enterprise', 'family', 'individual', 'personal', 'starter', 'plus']):
        print(f"  {k}: {v}")
PYEOF

# Good! So 1Password has: Personal, Family, Business, Enterprise, Small Teams
# Business is $9.99/mo per user (monthly) or $7.99/mo per user (annual)
# Enterprise is $11.99/mo per user (monthly) or $9.99/mo per user (annual)
# Small Teams is $24.95/mo for up to 10 users (annual) or $29.95/mo (monthly)

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import urllib.request, re

url = "https://bitwarden.com/pricing/"
req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36"})
html = urllib.request.urlopen(req).read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')

prices = re.findall(r'"price"\s*:\s*"([^"]*)"[^}]*"postPriceDetails"\s*:\s*"([^"]*)"', html)
if prices:
    for p, d in prices:
        print(f"  {p} — {d}")
else:
    # Try different pattern
    prices2 = re.findall(r'\$([0-9,]+(?:\.[0-9]+)?)\s*/\s*(?:mo|month|user|seat|yr|year)', html)
    print("Dollar amounts found:", prices2)
    
    # Try to find pricing in text
    text_prices = re.findall(r'(\$[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*(per\s*(?:user|seat|month|year)|/mo|/month|/user)', html, re.IGNORECASE)
    for p, u in text_prices:
        print(f"  {p} {u}")
PYEOF

# Let me try a different approach for LastPass - search for their pricing info
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import urllib.request, re

# Try LastPass pricing page with different approach
url = "https://lastpass.com/pricing/"
req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={
    "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36",
    "Accept": "text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8"
})
try:
    html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')
    
    # Look for pricing data in any format
    # Try to find JSON data
    json_blocks = re.findall(r'\{[^{}]*"price"[^{}]*\}', html)
    for j in json_blocks[:10]:
        print(j[:200])
    
    # Try to find any numeric pricing
    all_prices = re.findall(r'[\$€£]\s*[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?\s*(?:/|per|mo|month|user|seat|yr|year|annually)', html, re.IGNORECASE)
    print("\nPrice mentions:", all_prices[:20])
    
    # Look for plan names in data attributes
    plans = re.findall(r'data-[a-z]+="([^"]*(?:Business|Enterprise|Teams|Premium|Plus)[^"]*)"', html, re.IGNORECASE)
    print("Data-attribute plans:", set(plans[:20]))
    
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error: {e}")
PYEOF

(Free|Premium|Families|Teams|Enterprise|Business|Individual|Essentials|Standard|Advanced|Plus|Professional)

', html) print("Plans found:", set(plans)) # Find pricing context matches = re.findall(r'.{0,100}\$[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?.{0,100}', html) for m in matches[:15]: m = m.replace('\n', ' ') print(m[:200]) except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}") PYEOF

# Dashlane shows $11 for business. Let me get more detail
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import urllib.request, re

url = "https://www.dashlane.com/pricing"
req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36"})
html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')

# Find all pricing-related JSON
pricing_blocks = re.findall(r'"segment":"([^"]*)"[^}]*"customPrice":"([^"]*)"', html)
for seg, price in pricing_blocks:
    print(f"  {seg}: {price}")

# Also look for plan names
plan_names = re.findall(r'"name":"([^"]*)"[^}]*"segment"', html)
print("\nPlan names:", plan_names)

# Look for per-user pricing
per_user = re.findall(r'.{0,50}per.{0,50}user.{0,50}', html, re.IGNORECASE)
for p in per_user[:5]:
    print(f"  {p.replace(chr(10), ' ')}")
PYEOF

# Let me get the full pricing structure from Dashlane
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import urllib.request, re

url = "https://www.dashlane.com/pricing"
req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36"})
html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')

# Find all price values in the JSON
all_prices = re.findall(r'"price":\s*"([^"]*)"', html)
print("All 'price' values:", set(all_prices))

# Find customPrice
custom_prices = re.findall(r'"customPrice":\s*"([^"]*)"', html)
print("All 'customPrice' values:", set(custom_prices))

# Find segment names
segments = re.findall(r'"segment":\s*"([^"]*)"', html)
print("All 'segment' values:", set(segments))

# Find plan titles
titles = re.findall(r'"title":\s*"([^"]*)"', html)
print("All 'title' values:", set(titles))

# Find unitText
units = re.findall(r'"unitText":\s*"([^"]*)"', html)
print("All 'unitText' values:", set(units))
PYEOF

# Dashlane pricing is mostly "Custom" / "$11" - let me look at the HTML more carefully python3 << 'PYEOF' import urllib.request, re url = "https://www.dashlane.com/pricing" req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36"}) html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace') # Look for pricing cards cards = re.findall(r'class="[^"]*card[^"]*"[^>]*>.*?\s*', html, re.DOTALL) print(f"Found {len(cards)} card-like elements") # Look for plan names in headings headings = re.findall(r']*>(.*?)', html, re.DOTALL) for h in headings: h = h.strip().replace('\n', ' ').replace('&', '&') if h.strip(): print(f" H: {h[:100]}") # Look for "Personal", "Premium", "Business", "Enterprise" for term in ['Personal', 'Premium', 'Business', 'Enterprise', 'Teams', 'Family']: count = html.count(term) if count > 0: print(f" '{term}' appears {count} times") PYEOF

# Let me look at the actual pricing numbers in the HTML
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import urllib.request, re

url = "https://www.dashlane.com/pricing"
req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36"})
html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')

# Find all numbers near "per user" or "per month"
matches = re.findall(r'(\$?[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*(?:/|per\s+)?(?:user|month|mo|yr|year)', html, re.IGNORECASE)
print("Price-per-user/month mentions:", set(matches))

# Look for pricing in data attributes
data_prices = re.findall(r'data-price="([^"]*)"', html)
print("data-price:", set(data_prices))

# Look for price in any attribute
all_price_attrs = re.findall(r'[\w-]*price[\w-]*="([^"]*)"', html, re.IGNORECASE)
print("All price attributes:", set(all_price_attrs))

# Look for the actual pricing cards - search for $ signs in context
dollar_contexts = re.findall(r'.{0,50}\$[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?.{0,50}', html)
for ctx in dollar_contexts[:10]:
    ctx = ctx.replace('\n', ' ').replace('&', '&').replace('"', '"')
    print(f"  {ctx[:150]}")
PYEOF

import urllib.request, re

# Try to get pricing from a comparison site

    "https://www.g2.com/products/bitwarden-vs-1password",

]

# 1Password - get security/compliance info
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import urllib.request, re

# 1Password security
url = "https://1password.com/security/"
req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36"})
html = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10).read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')

# Look for compliance mentions
for term in ['SOC 2', 'FedRAMP', 'ISO 27001', 'ISO 27017', 'ISO 27018', 'penetration test', 'zero-knowledge', 'zero knowledge']:
    count = html.lower().count(term.lower())
    if count > 0:
        print(f"  1Password '{term}': {count} mentions")

# 1Password trust page
url2 = "https://1password.com/trust/"
req2 = urllib.request.Request(url2, headers={"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36"})
html2 = urllib.request.urlopen(req2, timeout=10).read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')
print(f"\n1Password trust page length: {len(html2)}")
for term in ['SOC 2', 'FedRAMP', 'ISO 27001', 'ISO 27017', 'ISO 27018', 'penetration test', 'audit']:
    count = html2.lower().count(term.lower())
    if count > 0:
        print(f"  Trust page '{term}': {count} mentions")
PYEOF

# Good! Bitwarden has SOC 2, ISO 27001, penetration testing, zero-knowledge
# Now let me research LastPass, Dashlane, and Keeper more thoroughly
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import urllib.request, re

def fetch(url):
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36"})
    try:
        return urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"  Error fetching {url}: {e}")
        return ""

# LastPass - Business
print("=== LastPass Business ===")
html = fetch("https://lastpass.com/business/")

def fetch(url):
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36"})

import urllib.request, re

def fetch(url):
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36"})
    try:
        return urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')
    except Exception as e:
        return ""

html = fetch("https://www.keepersecurity.com/pricing/business-and-enterprise.html")

# Look for the priceCards section
cards = re.search(r'id="priceCards".*?(?=</section|<footer)', html, re.DOTALL)
if cards:
    section = cards.group()
    # Extract all text
    text = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', ' ', section)
    text = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', text)
    print(f"Price cards text: {text[:1000]}")
    
    # Look for numbers
    numbers = re.findall(r'\d+(?:\.\d+)?', text)
    print(f"Numbers: {numbers}")

# Also look for pricing in the page's meta tags or structured data
meta = re.findall(r'<meta[^>]*content="([^"]*)"[^>]*>', html)
for m in meta:
    if 'price' in m.lower() or 'cost' in m.lower():
        print(f"Meta: {m}")

# Look for JSON-LD structured data
json_ld = re.findall(r'<script type="application/ld\+json">(.*?)</script>', html, re.DOTALL)
for j in json_ld:
    print(f"JSON-LD: {j[:500]}")

import urllib.request, re

def fetch(url):
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36"})
    try:
        return urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')
    except Exception as e:
        return ""

# LastPass pricing - look for React component data
html = fetch("https://lastpass.com/pricing/")

# Look for pricing in the page's data
# Search for pricing tile data
tiles = re.findall(r'LP(Business|Teams|Families|Premium|Professional|Individual)[^<]*', html)
print(f"LP tiles: {tiles}")

# Look for pricing in the page's JSON
json_data = re.findall(r'window\.__PRELOADED_STATE__\s*=\s*(\{.*?\});', html, re.DOTALL)
if json_data:
    print(f"Preloaded state found: {json_data[0][:500]}")

import urllib.request, re

def fetch(url):
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36"})
    try:
        return urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')
    except Exception as e:
        return ""

html = fetch("https://lastpass.com/pricing/")

# Look for pricing tile structure
# The page uses LPFamilies, LPTeams, LPBusiness, LPBusinessSSOMFA
# Let me look for the actual pricing numbers in the HTML

# Search for numbers near "per user" or "per month"
matches = re.findall(r'(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*(?:/|per\s+)?(?:user|month|mo|yr|year)', html, re.IGNORECASE)
print(f"Price-per-user/month: {set(matches)}")

# Look for pricing in the page's CSS classes
price_classes = re.findall(r'class="[^"]*price[^"]*"', html)
print(f"Price classes: {price_classes[:10]}")

# Look for pricing in the page's data attributes
data_prices = re.findall(r'data-[a-z-]*="([^"]*[\d]+[^"]*)"', html)
for dp in data_prices:
    if any(c.isdigit() for c in dp) and len(dp) < 50:
        print(f"  Data: {dp}")

# Look for pricing in the page's text content
# Extract all text and search for pricing
text = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', ' ', html)
text = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', text)
# Search for pricing patterns
pricing_patterns = re.findall(r'(\$?[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*(?:per|/)\s*(?:user|month|mo|yr|year|seat)', text, re.IGNORECASE)
print(f"\nPricing patterns: {pricing_patterns[:20]}")

import urllib.request, re

def fetch(url):
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36"})
    try:
        return urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')
    except Exception as e:
        return ""

html = fetch("https://www.tomsguide.com/best-picks/best-password-managers")
text = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', ' ', html)
text = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', text)

# Search for each product's pricing
for product in ['1Password', 'Bitwarden', 'LastPass', 'Dashlane', 'Keeper', 'NordPass']:
    idx = text.lower().find(product.lower())
    if idx > 0:
        context = text[max(0, idx-100):idx+300]
        prices = re.findall(r'\$[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?', context)
        if prices:
            print(f"\n{product}: {prices}")
            # Get more context
            context2 = text[max(0, idx-50):idx+500]
            print(f"  {context2[:400]}")

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import urllib.request, re

def fetch(url):
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36"})
    try:
        return urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')
    except Exception as e:
        return ""

# Dashlane pricing page
html = fetch("https://www.dashlane.com/pricing")

# Look for pricing in the page's JavaScript
scripts = re.findall(r'<script[^>]*>(.*?)</script>', html, re.DOTALL)
for s in scripts:
    if 'price' in s.lower() and len(s) > 100:
        # Look for pricing data
        prices = re.findall(r'\$[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?', s)
        if prices:
            print(f"Script prices: {prices}")
            print(f"Context: {s[:500]}")

# Also look for pricing in the page's JSON
json_data = re.findall(r'window\.__PRELOADED_STATE__\s*=\s*(\{.*?\});', html, re.DOTALL)
if json_data:
    print(f"Preloaded state found: {json_data[0][:500]}")

# Look for pricing in the page's data attributes
data_prices = re.findall(r'data-[a-z-]*price[a-z-]*="([^"]*)"', html, re.IGNORECASE)
print(f"Data price attrs: {data_prices}")
PYEOF

# Dashlane shows $11 as the only price. Let me look at the full JSON to find more pricing
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import urllib.request, re, json

def fetch(url):
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36"})
    try:
        return urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15).read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')
    except Exception as e:
        return ""

html = fetch("https://www.dashlane.com/pricing")

# Look for the full JSON data
json_data = re.findall(r'window\.__PRELOADED_STATE__\s*=\s*(\{.*?\});', html, re.DOTALL)
if json_data:
    data = json_data[0]
    # Look for pricing in the JSON
    # Search for all price-related fields
    price_fields = re.findall(r'"[^"]*price[^"]*"\s*:\s*"([^"]*)"', data, re.IGNORECASE)
    print(f"Price fields: {set(price_fields)}")
    
    # Search for all cost-related fields
    cost_fields = re.findall(r'"[^"]*cost[^"]*"\s*:\s*"([^"]*)"', data, re.IGNORECASE)
    print(f"Cost fields: {set(cost_fields)}")
    
    # Search for all amount-related fields
    amount_fields = re.findall(r'"[^"]*amount[^"]*"\s*:\s*"([^"]*)"', data, re.IGNORECASE)
    print(f"Amount fields: {set(amount_fields)}")
    
    # Search for plan names
    plan_names = re.findall(r'"[^"]*plan[^"]*"\s*:\s*"([^"]*)"', data, re.IGNORECASE)
    print(f"Plan names: {set(plan_names)}")
    
    # Search for tier names
    tier_names = re.findall(r'"[^"]*tier[^"]*"\s*:\s*"([^"]*)"', data, re.IGNORECASE)
    print(f"Tier names: {set(tier_names)}")
PYEOF
